text:
Once upon an autumn evening, owl biologist Lynn traveled deep into the forest. She'd always loved this particular owl nesting hollow. Tonight, though, she had a serious find to make.

Near the old oak she'd never heard chatter from. A clutch of fresh owl feathers... Could it be mine?

She picked up a feather here, an owl owl there. Yes! Ancient owl feathers. She'd find an owl. Mayberitch owl.

After hours of searching, she found him. Just there! There he perched. No big owl. Just a tired little snowy.

She named him Tiny. Tiny would tell stories. She knew owls. She knew about dreams.

So she'd chase him nights. And Tiny'd fly with her. They'd weave through starry nights. She'd pretend.

Eventually, owl Owls came. They didn. They didn't care. So she kept him.

They came often. So she knew. They brought berries.

One late winter, snow came.
